K-MILL.name: Mill-limited (the classic)
K-MILL.expectedBand: the mill binds → the cut-off is raised above break-even by f+F·δ; high-grading pays
K-MILL.validationAnchor: mean cut-off > break-even; the cut-off declines over the life

K-MINE.name: Mine-limited
K-MINE.expectedBand: mining binds → mill time is free → the cut-off sits near break-even

K-MARKET.name: Market-limited
K-MARKET.expectedBand: the market binds → the cut-off is raised to lift the average grade
K-MARKET.validationAnchor: mean cut-off > break-even; market is the binding stage early

S-HIGHPRICE.name: High price (+40%)
S-HIGHPRICE.expectedBand: higher price → lower break-even → more is ore → higher NPV

S-LOWPRICE.name: Low price (−30%)
S-LOWPRICE.expectedBand: lower price → higher break-even → less is ore → lower NPV

D-HIVAR.name: High-variability deposit (CV 1.0)
D-HIVAR.expectedBand: a fat grade tail → high-grading earns a lot → a strong declining cut-off

D-LOWVAR.name: Low-variability deposit (CV 0.2)
D-LOWVAR.expectedBand: a tight grade band → the cut-off barely matters → little high-grading uplift

C-UNIFORM.name: Oracle — single-grade deposit
C-UNIFORM.expectedBand: CV→0: all-or-nothing. The mean grade is above break-even → everything is ore; NPV is closed-form
C-UNIFORM.validationAnchor: ore fraction is 1 below the grade and 0 above; NPV matches the closed form

C-BREAKEVEN.name: Oracle — no time cost (break-even)
C-BREAKEVEN.expectedBand: f=0, δ=0, mine-limited → no opportunity cost → the optimal cut-off = the break-even h/((p−k)·y)
